terms.definition: A license model that tracks concurrent usage per day. Unlimited users can be added to the license group, but only a purchased number can be active in a single day. The license resets at 23:59:59 GMT.
A system-generated database group that corresponds to a context team role. Used by the Policy Administrator to apply access control rules to participants assigned to that role.
A user (typically a product or project manager) who indirectly manages access control and workflow tasks by assigning participants to team roles within a context.
A role visible in the Members table of a context team page. Custom context roles can be created for access control but are not available for workflow or lifecycle role resolution.
A team defined at the sub-organization level (product, project, library, or program) that manages context roles and context groups for access control and business process role resolution.
An enterprise LDAP server that stores participant information shared across applications, such as names, email addresses, phone numbers, group membership, and organization affiliation.
A user or group object in Windchill that no longer points to a valid entry in the directory server, typically caused by deletion from the directory server without corresponding Windchill cleanup.
A configurable list of values used throughout Windchill, such as roles and lifecycle states. Custom entries can be added using the Customize Modeled Enumerations utility.
A site-level group that enables a user to be licensed without being counted against the purchased license total. Used for testing and administrative purposes.
A group that assigns a specific Windchill license to its members. Each license package has a corresponding license group that users must be added to for access.
A PTC-maintained profile that controls the visibility of UI actions and features based on license functionality. Each license group has a corresponding license profile.
A license model that limits the number of specific users who can be added to a license group. When the license count is at capacity, no additional users can be added.
A rule that defines which lifecycle template and team template are applied when a new object of a specific type is created in a given context.
A participant type that acts as an administrative domain containing users and groups. Most Windchill implementations use a single organization context for all users.
A generic term for organizations, groups, and users in Windchill. Participant data is split between a directory server (shared attributes) and the Windchill database (Windchill-specific data).
A Windchill utility that enables business administrators to manage user accounts, groups, and organizations including creation, editing, and deletion.
A private storage area for each Windchill user that contains server-side workspaces used by CAD and Arbortext applications. Must be deleted separately when removing a user account.
A role defined in the system's resource bundle files that is available to workflow, lifecycle, and team template administration tools. Over 180 are provided out of the box.
A standard title or function used to map participants to business responsibilities. Roles are not job titles — they define access control and workflow task assignments.
A team defined at the organization level that can be used across multiple products, libraries, projects, or programs. Changes to a shared team are reflected in all associated contexts.
A customizable profile that can hide licensed features from users. Standard profiles restrict visibility but cannot grant features beyond a user's license entitlement.
A system-generated team for a specific business object that resolves roles from the team template, lifecycle template, and context team. Once created, it is unaffected by template updates.
A hidden context group that contains all participants added to any context role except Guest. Visible only in the Policy Administration utility, not on the team page.
A template that defines roles for business processes and advanced workflow resolution. Created at the site, organization, or application context level and assigned via object initialization rules.
